Name: rate_distortion_optimal_quantization
Title: Rate-Distortion Optimal Quantization for Neural Network Compression
Short Hypothesis: By formulating model compression as a rate-distortion problem, we can derive optimal quantization strategies that balance the trade-off between model size and performance, leading to more efficient and effective compression schemes.
Related Work: Recent works such as QuEST [1] and Prob-AMC [7] have explored quantization and compression from an information-theoretic perspective. However, these methods often focus on specific aspects like selective finetuning or mutual information optimization. Our proposal aims to provide a more comprehensive rate-distortion framework that unifies these approaches and derives optimal quantization strategies under a unified theoretical framework.
Abstract: We propose a novel method for neural network compression that formulates the problem as a rate-distortion optimization. By treating the model parameters as a source to be compressed and the model's performance as a distortion measure, we derive optimal quantization schemes that balance the trade-off between model size and accuracy. Our approach leverages information-theoretic tools such as entropy, mutual information, and rate-distortion theory to provide a principled framework for model compression. We derive achievable and converse bounds for the rate-distortion trade-off and propose efficient algorithms to implement these schemes. Experimental results on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art compression ratios with minimal performance degradation, outperforming existing methods in both compression efficiency and accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Derive the theoretical rate-distortion function for neural network parameters using entropy and mutual information.
Experiments.Method: Use entropy estimators and mutual information lower bounds (e.g., InfoNCE) to quantify the information content of the model parameters and their relationship to the task performance.

Experiments.Description: Develop an algorithm to achieve the derived rate-distortion trade-off.
Experiments.Method: Implement a gradient-based optimization algorithm that jointly minimizes the rate (model size) and distortion (performance degradation). Use techniques such as proximal gradient descent and Lagrange multipliers to enforce the rate-distortion constraint.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the proposed method on standard benchmarks.
Experiments.Method: Test the compression and performance of the proposed method on datasets such as CIFAR-10 and ImageNet. Compare the compression ratios and accuracy with state-of-the-art methods like QuEST and Prob-AMC.

Experiments.Description: Analyze the impact of different hyperparameters on the rate-distortion trade-off.
Experiments.Method: Vary hyperparameters such as the distortion threshold and the learning rate to study their effects on the compression and performance. Use cross-validation to select the optimal hyperparameters.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The theoretical derivation of the rate-distortion function may require strong assumptions about the distribution of model parameters and the task performance.
The optimization algorithm may be computationally expensive, especially for large models and datasets.
The method may not generalize well to all types of neural networks and tasks, particularly those with highly non-linear or adversarial properties.
